metadata.title: SWAG Post-Training + Comprehensive Calibration Metrics Implementation
metadata.priority: P0 — publication gate for Nature Protocols manuscript

context.summary:
Two-phase implementation: Phase A: SWAG as THE post-training factor for publication factorial design Phase B: ALL calibration metrics in the Analysis Flow (+ fast subset in Train flow)
SWAG (Maddox et al. 2019) replaces the mislabeled "SWA" checkpoint averaging. Calibration metrics validate the with_aux_calib factorial factor.
Both phases use self-learning-iterative-coder TDD skill.

existing-infrastructure.fact: PostTrainingConfig has 6 plugin configs with enabled toggles
CalibrationPlugin already does global temperature, isotonic, spatial Platt
ensemble/calibration.py has expected_calibration_error(confidences, accuracies, n_bins) returning (ECE, MCE)
pipeline/calibration.py has MetaTemperatureNetwork for per-pixel temperature
MultiMetricTracker tracks 6 best-metric checkpoints per run
SegmentationMetrics computes only Dice + F1 (no calibration metrics)
EvaluationRunner computes per-volume metrics via FoldResult

phase.name: Rename SWA → Checkpoint Averaging
phase.description: Rename all mislabeled "SWA" references to "checkpoint averaging". ~18 files affected. Pure renaming — no logic changes. See: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-21-fake-swa-checkpoint-averaging-mislabeled.md

tasks.task: Files: - src/minivess/pipeline/post_training_plugins/swa.py → checkpoint_averaging.py - Class SWAPlugin → CheckpointAveragingPlugin - Property name → "checkpoint_averaging"
Files: - src/minivess/pipeline/post_training_plugins/multi_swa.py → subsampled_ensemble.py - Class MultiSWAPlugin → SubsampledEnsemblePlugin - Property name → "subsampled_ensemble"
Files: - src/minivess/ensemble/model_soup.py: uniform_swa → uniform_checkpoint_average - src/minivess/ensemble/model_soup.py: swa_from_checkpoints → average_checkpoints
Files: - src/minivess/config/post_training_config.py: swa → checkpoint_averaging, multi_swa → subsampled_ensemble - Update FactorialMethod enum values
Files: - src/minivess/orchestration/flows/post_training_flow.py: _WEIGHT_PLUGINS, imports, swa_completed field - Update run_factorial_post_training method names

Files: - configs/factorial/*.yaml: method levels - configs/post_training/default.yaml: config keys - tests/v2/unit/test_swa_plugin.py → test_checkpoint_averaging_plugin.py - tests/v2/unit/test_multi_swa_plugin.py → test_subsampled_ensemble_plugin.py - tests/v2/unit/test_model_soup_swa.py → test_model_soup_averaging.py

phase.gate: All tests pass after rename. grep -r "SWAPlugin\|MultiSWAPlugin\|uniform_swa" src/ returns 0 hits.

phase.name: Implement SWAG Post-Training Plugin
phase.description:
Implement real SWAG (Maddox et al. 2019) as a post-training plugin. Uses torch.optim.swa_utils for the SWA phase + custom second-moment tracking.
SWAG requires RESUMED TRAINING — this is the key difference from checkpoint averaging. The plugin loads a trained model + training DataLoaders, then: 1. Resumes training with SWALR for N additional epochs 2. Tracks first moments (running mean via AveragedModel) 3. Tracks second moments (running squared mean + low-rank deviations) 4. Recalibrates batch normalization via update_bn() 5. Saves SWAG model (mean + covariance parameters)
Reference implementation: github.com/wjmaddox/swa_gaussian PyTorch utils: torch.optim.swa_utils.AveragedModel, SWALR, update_bn

tasks.task:
File: src/minivess/config/post_training_config.py
class SWAGConfig(BaseModel): enabled: bool = False swa_lr: float = 0.01 # From config, NOT hardcoded swa_epochs: int = 10 # Additional epochs after training max_rank: int = 20 # Low-rank covariance approximation rank n_samples: int = 30 # Number of posterior samples at inference update_bn: bool = True seed: int (from parent config)
Add to PostTrainingConfig.swag field.

File: src/minivess/ensemble/swag.py
class SWAGModel: """SWAG posterior approximation (Maddox et al. 2019).
Wraps torch.optim.swa_utils.AveragedModel with additional second-moment tracking for Gaussian posterior approximation. """ def __init__(self, base_model: nn.Module, max_rank: int = 20) def collect_model(self, model: nn.Module) -> None # Track mean, sq_mean, and low-rank deviations def sample(self, scale: float = 1.0, seed: int | None = None) -> None # Sample from Gaussian posterior: w ~ N(mean, (1/2) * Sigma_diag + (1/2K) * D @ D.T) def forward(self, x: Tensor) -> Tensor def save(self, path: Path) -> None def load(cls, path: Path, base_model: nn.Module) -> SWAGModel
Use torch.optim.swa_utils.AveragedModel for the mean tracking. Custom tracking for sq_mean and deviation vectors. Reference: wjmaddox/swa_gaussian/swag/posteriors/swag.py

File: src/minivess/pipeline/post_training_plugins/swag.py
class SWAGPlugin(PostTrainingPlugin): name = "swag" requires_calibration_data = True # Needs training DataLoaders
def validate_inputs(self, plugin_input: PluginInput) -> list[str]: # Requires: checkpoint_paths, calibration_data (DataLoaders) # calibration_data must have 'train_loader' key
def execute(self, plugin_input: PluginInput) -> PluginOutput: # 1. Load base model from checkpoint # 2. Create optimizer with SWALR schedule # 3. Resume training for swa_epochs # 4. Collect SWAG statistics each epoch # 5. update_bn(train_loader, swag_model) # 6. Save SWAG model (mean + covariance) # Return: model_paths=[swag_model_path], metrics={swag_epochs, swag_lr}

Add SWAGPlugin to _build_registry(). Add "swag" to _WEIGHT_PLUGINS (it needs calibration_data but is weight-based). Actually: SWAG needs DataLoaders → _DATA_PLUGINS. Update _DATA_PLUGINS = {"swag", "calibration", "crc_conformal", "conseco_fp_control"}.

task.title: Write SWAG inference integration for Analysis Flow

tasks.task:
The Analysis Flow needs to know how to load a SWAG model and sample from it. Modify _extract_single_models_as_modules or create _extract_swag_models that loads the SWAG model, samples K models, wraps in _EnsembleInferenceWrapper.
File: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/analysis_flow.py
configs/factorial/smoke_local.yaml: method: [none, swag] configs/factorial/debug.yaml: method: [none, swag] configs/factorial/paper_full.yaml: method: [none, swag]

phase.gate: SWAG plugin creates valid SWAG model from DynUNet checkpoint. SWAG model produces K diverse posterior samples. update_bn recalibrates batch normalization. All existing tests pass.

phase.name: SWAG Tests
phase.description: TDD — write tests FIRST (RED), then implement (GREEN).

tasks.task: tests/v2/unit/test_swag_model.py: - test_swag_collect_updates_mean_and_sq_mean - test_swag_sample_produces_different_weights - test_swag_save_load_roundtrip - test_swag_low_rank_covariance_shape - test_swag_bn_update_changes_running_stats
tests/v2/unit/test_swag_plugin.py: - test_swag_plugin_validate_requires_calibration_data - test_swag_plugin_execute_produces_model_file - test_swag_plugin_metrics_logged

task.title: Integration test for SWAG in factorial pipeline

tasks.task: tests/v2/integration/test_swag_factorial.py: - test_factorial_with_swag_method (uses debug DynUNet, 1 epoch SWAG)

phase.gate: All SWAG tests pass. make test-staging green.

phase.name: Calibration Metrics Module
phase.description:
Create comprehensive calibration metrics module with ALL metrics from the literature report. Organized into FAST (training loop) and COMPREHENSIVE (analysis flow) tiers.
Library-first: use torchmetrics, SDC-Loss repo, netcal, properscoring wherever possible. Custom implementation only for spatial metrics (BA-ECE, SPACE, BUC) and pECE (not in standard libraries for segmentation).
ALL metrics. No subset. No resistance.

tasks.task:
File: src/minivess/pipeline/calibration_metrics.py
## Tier 1: FAST metrics (O(N), suitable for training val loop) def compute_ece(probs, labels, n_bins=15) -> float def compute_mce(probs, labels, n_bins=15) -> float def compute_rmsce(probs, labels, n_bins=15) -> float def compute_brier_score(probs, labels) -> float def compute_nll(probs, labels) -> float # Binary CE def compute_buc(probs, labels, boundary_width=3) -> float def compute_cece(probs, labels, n_bins=10) -> float # C=2 for binary def compute_overconfidence_error(probs, labels, n_bins=15) -> float # OE def compute_debiased_ece(probs, labels, n_bins=15) -> float # D-ECE (Kumar 2019) def compute_smooth_ece(probs, labels) -> float # smECE (Blasiok 2024)
## Tier 2: COMPREHENSIVE metrics (analysis flow only) def compute_pece(probs, labels, n_bins=10, w_fp=1.0) -> float # Pixel-wise ECE with FP penalty (Li et al. 2025, SDC-Loss) def compute_ace(probs, labels, n_bins=20) -> float # Average Calibration Error — adaptive equal-count bins (Nixon et al. 2019) def compute_tace(probs, labels, n_bins=20, threshold=0.01) -> float # Thresholded Adaptive CE (Nixon et al. 2019) def compute_sce(probs, labels, n_bins=15) -> float # Static Calibration Error — full confidence vector (Nixon et al. 2019) def compute_ba_ece(probs, labels, n_bands=10) -> float # Boundary-Aware ECE (Zeevi et al. 2025) # Uses scipy.ndimage.distance_transform_edt def compute_space(probs, labels, sigma=3.0) -> float # Spatially-Aware Calibration Error (Zeevi et al. 2025) # Uses Gaussian convolution def compute_crps(probs, labels) -> float # Continuous Ranked Probability Score (CURVAS challenge metric) def compute_uncertainty_dice_auc(probs, labels, thresholds=None) -> float # QU-BraTS style: DSC after removing top-K% uncertain voxels
## Spatial maps (return per-voxel arrays, not scalars) def compute_brier_map(probs, labels) -> NDArray def compute_nll_map(probs, labels) -> NDArray
## Convenience: compute ALL at once def compute_all_calibration_metrics( probs: Tensor, labels: Tensor, *, tier: Literal["fast", "comprehensive"] = "comprehensive", ) -> dict[str, float]

task.title: Write tests for ALL calibration metrics (RED phase)

tasks.task:
File: tests/v2/unit/test_calibration_metrics.py
For EACH metric: - test_perfect_calibration (probs=labels → metric≈0 or ≈1 for BUC) - test_worst_calibration (probs=1-labels → metric≈max) - test_known_values (synthetic data with known ECE) - test_output_range (0 ≤ metric ≤ 1 or appropriate range) - test_batch_independence (result same regardless of batch split)
For spatial metrics additionally: - test_boundary_aware (BA-ECE higher at boundaries) - test_spatial_correlation (SPACE detects spatial patterns)

phase.gate: All calibration metric tests pass. Each metric produces correct values on synthetic data.

phase.name: Integrate Calibration Metrics into Training Validation
phase.description: Add Tier 1 (FAST) calibration metrics to the training validation loop. Computed every val_interval epochs alongside existing Dice + F1. Must NOT slow down validation by more than 10%.

task.title: Add fast calibration metrics to SegmentationMetrics

tasks.task:
File: src/minivess/pipeline/metrics.py
Extend SegmentationMetrics.compute() to include: - ECE (via torchmetrics.BinaryCalibrationError, GPU) - MCE (via torchmetrics.BinaryCalibrationError(norm='max'), GPU) - Brier Score (simple MSE, GPU) - NLL (already computed as CE loss — log it) - BUC (requires distance transform — compute on CPU after GPU metrics)
All metrics logged to MLflow as val_ece, val_mce, val_brier, val_nll, val_buc.

File: src/minivess/pipeline/multi_metric_tracker.py
Add trackers for val_ece (MINIMIZE), val_brier (MINIMIZE). Do NOT save additional checkpoints for calibration metrics (keep 7 existing). Just track for logging and early stopping awareness.

tests/v2/unit/test_metrics_calibration.py: - test_ece_computed_during_validation - test_brier_computed_during_validation - test_calibration_metrics_logged_to_mlflow - test_calibration_metrics_do_not_slow_validation (benchmark)

phase.gate: Training validation loop computes 5 calibration metrics. Validation time increase < 10%. Metrics logged to MLflow.

phase.name: Integrate ALL Calibration Metrics into Analysis Flow
phase.description:
Add ALL calibration metrics (Tier 1 + Tier 2) to the Analysis Flow evaluation. These are computed once per model on the full validation/test set. Computational cost is acceptable since this runs once, not every epoch.
Also compute calibration metrics on BOTH MiniVess (in-distribution) and DeepVess (out-of-distribution) to measure calibration shift.

tasks.task:
File: src/minivess/pipeline/evaluation_runner.py
After computing segmentation predictions, also compute: - ALL Tier 1 metrics: ECE, MCE, RMSCE, Brier, NLL, BUC - ALL Tier 2 metrics: pECE, ACE, CECE, TACE, BA-ECE, SPACE, CRPS, Uncertainty-Dice AUC - Spatial maps: Brier map, NLL map (saved as .npz artifacts)
Log to MLflow with prefix: eval/{fold}/{metric} (e.g., eval/fold_0/ece) For external test: test/{dataset}/{metric} (e.g., test/deepvess/ece)

After computing calibration metrics on both MiniVess and DeepVess, compute delta metrics: - delta_ece = ece_deepvess - ece_minivess - delta_brier = brier_deepvess - brier_minivess - etc.
Log as: calibration_shift/{metric} (e.g., calibration_shift/ece)

Generate per-model reliability diagrams (accuracy vs confidence, 15 bins) using the Average-Calibration-Losses repo's ReliabilityDiagramMetric.
Save as MLflow artifacts: figures/reliability_diagram_{model_name}.png

tests/v2/unit/test_analysis_calibration.py: - test_all_calibration_metrics_computed_in_analysis - test_calibration_shift_computed - test_reliability_diagram_generated - test_brier_map_shape_matches_volume

phase.gate: Analysis flow computes ALL calibration metrics for each model. Calibration shift computed between MiniVess and DeepVess. Reliability diagrams saved as artifacts.

phase.name: Biostatistics Integration
phase.description: Add calibration metrics to the biostatistics factorial ANOVA. The with_aux_calib factor can now be tested against calibration metrics, not just DSC/clDice.

tasks.task:
File: src/minivess/config/biostatistics_config.py
Add calibration metrics to default metrics list: metrics: [dsc, cldice, hd95, assd, nsd, be_0, be_1, junction_f1, ece, mce, brier, pece, ace, ba_ece]

task.title: Verify ANOVA on with_aux_calib × calibration metrics

tasks.task: The factorial ANOVA should test: - H1: with_aux_calib=true reduces ECE (primary calibration hypothesis) - H2: with_aux_calib=true reduces pECE (false-positive calibration) - H3: with_aux_calib=true reduces BA-ECE (boundary calibration) - H4: DSC/clDice trade-off (accuracy may decrease slightly)

phase.gate: Biostatistics ANOVA includes calibration metrics. Test suite passes.

phase.name: Final Verification

tasks.task: All 5598+ tests must pass. 0 new skips.

task.title: Local 3-flow smoke test with SWAG + calibration

tasks.task: Run scripts/run_local_debug_3flow.py with updated factorial design. Verify SWAG produces K posterior samples. Verify calibration metrics computed and logged.
Single PR to main with all Phase A + Phase B changes.

phase.gate: make test-staging passes. Local smoke test validates full pipeline.

execution-notes.note: Library-first: torchmetrics, SDC-Loss repo, netcal, properscoring before custom code
Fetch SDC-Loss and Average-Calibration-Losses repos before implementing their metrics
SWAG needs DataLoaders — update plugin contract for data-dependent plugins
ALL calibration metrics means ALL — no subset, no resistance
Computational slowness is ONLY a concern for Tier 1 (training loop), NOT Tier 2 (analysis)
BUC and BA-ECE need distance transform — already available from clDice/MASD computation

issue.title: feat: implement comprehensive calibration metrics module
issue.label: P0-critical,metrics

issue.title: feat: add fast calibration metrics (ECE, Brier) to training val loop
issue.label: P1-high,metrics

issue.title: feat: add ALL calibration metrics to Analysis Flow
issue.label: P0-critical,analysis

issue.title: feat: calibration shift computation (MiniVess vs DeepVess)
issue.label: P1-high,analysis
